textByHash.0b417c13ff17bdf5f2d725cd453cd3360512b8cb: That might be fun. I wouldn't want to be like gramps though. He liked wars a bit too much. I do sometimes discuss the Khazard War with the hobgobbos, but they don't have a particularly sharp political sense.
textByHash.1c1191ff70515aeef9d64be01ddeedcd82dbd11a: Not yet, but don't worry. I'm sure I'll work something out. I just need some time to think.
textByHash.29708078aac8765bb7c0f5b9caadd4512009f256: Of course! Bolrie is my grandfather... assuming he's still alive, at least.
textByHash.2e62b4381391443042730a2f89d2bd9914fc7dd7: Not to worry. It turned up here again.
textByHash.2ef59166da946c84cda14cfb0d6616108a569654: Oh that, yes have it, it's just some old elven junk I believe.
textByHash.312bd11618962899c24cf7bc42cb720fed866987: A huge battle tortoise arrived a while back carrying a crate with it in. Sent by gramps, of course. It looks pretty, doesn't it? Would probably look great with some plants in it.
textByHash.4315bc4ddf1b9926decea84280848a90710380f6: Thanks a lot for the key traveller. I think I'll wait in here until those hob-gobs get bored and leave.
textByHash.4608d129153bc5a2f4fb5f2a3fb1df31b6e96802: Wonderful! Though you know, I don't think you ever actually mentioned what the device does.
textByHash.4b28d42b1bd04e4dc16b7111dce7ccd0d556b345: Gnomes don't tend to dwell on the past, but I suppose I'm not a typical gnome. I get lots of time to think and read in here.
textByHash.5463cd4a2a4ee65fac783bca87d6e113f29cae92: It's just those blasted hob-gobs. I locked myself in here for protection. But I've left the key somewhere and now I'm stuck.
textByHash.5aed7f103e0a45dd4d1cd49604b6b4662d13acee: Ah, well I've got plenty of elven stuff. It's all lying around here somewhere.
textByHash.5e7669ab63c50960a764de3df9840c4392eead5e: If you wanna go in there with them hob-gobs, that's your business.
textByHash.6533a949875878cd7660a25e386f0652c2ebde67: Have a look in the other chamber and you'll find a small tunnel into the storeroom.
textByHash.7138094096d9ec60b56d749b9bb9be34441cfeee: Not me, my grandfather. At least, he used to be. I'm not sure whether he's still alive or not. He hasn't sent me any souvenirs for a while now.
textByHash.7b6150cf351c3fbb2ce46cf09c82399384051248: Certainly, in our family that's true. King Bolren is also Bolrie's younger brother, in case you didn't know.
textByHash.82f347e21388dd3371df0e29729f075b664c2d16: No, the parcels stopped arriving a few years ago. I guess he's either found what he was looking for, or has died in the attempt.
textByHash.85fb50cc6aeb9dea1ba95e05540a2f4bb81bc16a: What are you doing down here? Leave before you get yourself into trouble.
textByHash.935063360fb32f39baaa9372d131edd5552e0ee5: Oh, well I'll be sure to keep that in mind.
textByHash.9d191dec970e6e5333e9eccf8b0af3f11b433300: It used to be a monthly occurence. A terrorbird would turn up with a big parcel and I'd throw it in with the other stuff in the storeroom. It's a bit of a mess now in there.
textByHash.9db51a37b38d1484832678c18582545b8502fc87: I'm not sure. He was definitely obsessed with finding out as much as he could about elven magic. He also seemed to have a bit of a grudge against Glouphrie.
textByHash.baa33dfbce92a001485d3c8eede179197d2f8bb5: It just so happens I have. Take a look at this book.
textByHash.bb7098a7bd28f5fcae1af1f3142b92e395aa0856: That's right. He was a funny gnome, old gramps. He got into a bit of... trouble a while back and couldn't really stick around. He ended up travelling the world collecting elven artefacts after that.
textByHash.bfc19b451de39976bcaae8d83cb0eb272baba17e: Yes, lots to do. Them hob-gobs left my stuff out here in a right mess. Until next time.
textByHash.d4798f92bc7749f5a8256c2ff0f6c8f45a893a5a: Gnomes don't have a hereditary monarchy. We have a democratic monarchy. We vote for our kings.
textByHash.de216dcf05d889943479fe90c0e9d0371c51ca60: Personally, I don't think we should fight his troops head- on. We're smarter than that. We should come up with some ingenious method of defeating Khazard, like they did at the Battle of Atarisundri!
textByHash.e13e84d07d6e4edcb5dbee6da7f31cc8a5930e09: Better now I'm on the right side of these bars.
textByHash.e65fb114ab06f43f0bab8075acd86ebce0607dc8: Oh, certainly. I should really sort through all of his things at some point. There's probably some quite cool stuff in there.
textByHash.f028b1cdf761cf9b8e8efb305c6e7f20470a245c: I'm Golrie, I've been stuck in here for ages. those hob-gobs are trying to steal my family's heirlooms. My grandad gave me all sorts of old junk.
textByHash.ff6f6974e1935436fb509b44eb79dcbacec6b58f: Did you check the storeroom? You can get to it through the small tunnel in the other chamber.
